101.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda' that Abu al-Darda' used to smile when he narrated a hadith. She said to him: 'People will think you are foolish.' He said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß used to smile when he narrated hadith.'
101.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda' bahwa Abu al-Darda' biasa tersenyum ketika menceritakan sebuah hadith. Ia berkata kepadanya: 'Orang-orang akan mengira kamu bodoh.' Ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß biasa tersenyum ketika menceritakan hadith.'

102.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß said: "While I was sleeping, I saw a pillar of the Book being taken from under my head. I thought it was being taken away. I followed it with my eyes, and it was thrust into Syria. Truly, when the trials come, faith will be in Syria."'
102.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Ketika aku sedang tidur, aku melihat tiang Kitab diambil dari bawah kepalaku. Aku mengira ia akan pergi. Aku mengikutinya dengan mataku, dan ia ditempatkan di Syam. Sesungguhnya ketika fitnah terjadi, keimanan akan ada di Syam."'

103.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß said: "Revere Allah, and He will forgive you."' Ibn Thawban said this means: 'Submit (to Islam).'
103.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Agungkanlah Allah, maka Dia akan mengampunimu."' Ibn Tsauban berkata ini bermakna: 'Islamlah (masuk Islam).'

104.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm al-Darda' that she said: 'Abu al-Darda' never narrated a hadith without smiling while doing so. I said to him: "I fear that people will think you foolish." He said: "The Messenger of Allah ß used to smile when he narrated hadith."'
104.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm al-Darda' bahwa ia berkata: 'Abu al-Darda' tidak pernah menceritakan hadith tanpa tersenyum saat melakukannya. Aku berkata kepadanya: "Aku khawatir orang-orang akan mengira kamu bodoh." Ia berkata: "Rasulullah ß biasa tersenyum ketika menceritakan hadith."'

105.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was visited by someone who greeted him with health (saying 'with health, not with sickness') three times. He then said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ß say: "Whatever a Muslim is afflicted with, even a thorn that pricks him, Allah erases his sins thereby."'
105.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia dikunjungi oleh seseorang yang menyapanya dengan kesehatan (mengatakan 'dengan kesehatan, bukan dengan penyakit') sebanyak tiga kali. Kemudian ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Apapun yang menimpa seorang Muslim, bahkan duri yang menusuknya, Allah menghapus dosanya karenanya."'

106.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger ß said: "I am the first one who will be permitted to prostrate on the Day of Resurrection, and I am the first one who will be permitted to raise his head."'
106.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Aku adalah orang pertama yang akan diizinkan untuk bersujud pada Hari Kiamat, dan aku adalah orang pertama yang akan diizinkan untuk mengangkat kepalanya."'

107.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr and Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with them both) that the Messenger of Allah ß said: 'I shall recognize my nation on the Day of Resurrection among all the other nations by the whiteness of their faces from the traces of ablution.'
107.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dzar dan Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Aku akan mengenali umatku pada Hari Kiamat di antara semua umat lainnya dari putihnya wajah mereka karena bekas wudhu.'

108.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß said: 'None of you should refrain from praying for his brother in his absence, for there is an angel delegated to him who, whenever he prays good for his brother, says: Amen, and may you have the same.'
108.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Janganlah seorang pun dari kalian menahan diri dari mendoakan saudaranya saat ia tidak hadir, karena ada malaikat yang ditugaskan untuknya yang, setiap kali ia mendoakan kebaikan untuk saudaranya, berkata: Amin, dan semoga kamu mendapat hal yang sama.'

109.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that Kurayb asked him: 'Tell me what you did on the evening you rode behind the Messenger of Allah ß.' He said: 'We came to the mountain pass where the riders of Quraysh had made their camels kneel. The Messenger of Allah ß dismounted and urinated, then performed a light ablution. I said: "Prayer, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "The prayer is ahead of you." We then went on until we came to Muzdalifa, where he prayed Maghrib and Isha combined.'
109.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Kurayb bertanya kepadanya: 'Ceritakan kepadaku apa yang kamu lakukan pada malam kamu membonceng Rasulullah ß.' Ia berkata: 'Kami tiba di celah gunung tempat para pengendara Quraisy membarakkan unta-unta mereka. Rasulullah ß turun dan buang air kecil, kemudian berwudhu ringan. Aku berkata: "Shalat, wahai Rasulullah." Beliau bersabda: "Shalat ada di depanmu." Kami kemudian melanjutkan hingga tiba di Muzdalifah, di mana beliau shalat Maghrib dan Isya digabung.'

110.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß said: 'There is no riba (usury) in hand-to-hand exchange' — meaning that riba only applies to credit transactions.
110.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Tidak ada riba dalam transaksi tunai' — yakni riba hanya berlaku pada transaksi kredit (nasiah).'

111.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he used to travel to Wadi al-Qura to collect some money owed to him, and he fasted on Mondays and Thursdays. His servant asked him about fasting. He said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß used to fast Mondays and Thursdays. I asked him about that and he said: "Deeds are presented to Allah on Mondays and Thursdays."'
111.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia biasa bepergian ke Wadi al-Qura untuk mengambil sejumlah uang yang terutang kepadanya, dan ia berpuasa pada hari Senin dan Kamis. Pelayannya bertanya kepadanya tentang puasa. Ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß biasa berpuasa pada hari Senin dan Kamis. Aku bertanya kepadanya tentang hal itu dan beliau bersabda: "Amalan-amalan dipersembahkan kepada Allah pada hari Senin dan Kamis."'

112.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß sent us to al-Huraqa of Juhayna. We attacked them at dawn and defeated them. A man from among them, when he advanced against the Muslims, would say: "There is no god but Allah." One of the Ansar killed him, and when we came back, the Prophet ß was told. He said: "O so-and-so, did you kill him after he said: There is no god but Allah?" He said: "He only said it to save his life." The Prophet ß kept repeating: "Who will excuse you before Allah when you have killed a man who said: There is no god but Allah?"'
112.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß mengutus kami ke al-Huraqa dari Juhaynah. Kami menyerang mereka saat fajar dan mengalahkan mereka. Seorang pria dari antara mereka, ketika ia maju melawan kaum Muslim, berkata: "Tidak ada tuhan selain Allah." Salah seorang Anshar membunuhnya, dan ketika kami kembali, Nabi ß diberitahu. Beliau bersabda: "Wahai si fulan, apakah kamu membunuhnya setelah ia mengucapkan: Tidak ada tuhan selain Allah?" Ia berkata: "Ia hanya mengucapkannya untuk menyelamatkan nyawanya." Nabi ß terus mengulang: "Siapa yang akan membelamu di hadapan Allah ketika kamu membunuh seorang pria yang mengucapkan: Tidak ada tuhan selain Allah?"'

113.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß said: "I have not left behind me a trial more harmful to men than women."'
113.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Aku tidak meninggalkan setelahku fitnah yang lebih berbahaya bagi kaum pria daripada wanita."'

114.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß said: 'A Muslim does not inherit from a disbeliever, nor does a disbeliever inherit from a Muslim.'
114.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß bersabda: 'Seorang Muslim tidak mewarisi dari orang kafir, dan orang kafir tidak mewarisi dari orang Muslim.'

115.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß looked out over one of the fortresses of Madinah and said: 'Do you see what I see? Verily I see the sites of trials among your homes as (numerous as) the sites where rain falls.'
115.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß memandang keluar dari salah satu benteng Madinah dan bersabda: 'Apakah kalian melihat apa yang aku lihat? Sesungguhnya aku melihat tempat-tempat fitnah di antara rumah-rumah kalian sebanyak tempat-tempat jatuhnya hujan.'

116.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß carried him as a pillion from Arafat. When they reached the mountain pass, he dismounted and urinated — without saying he relieved himself — then I poured water over him, and he performed a brief ablution. I said: 'Prayer, O Messenger of Allah.' He said: 'Prayer is ahead of you.'
116.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß membawanya berbonceng dari Arafat. Ketika mereka tiba di celah gunung, beliau turun dan buang air kecil — tanpa berkata bahwa beliau membuang air — kemudian aku menuangkan air kepadanya, dan beliau berwudhu singkat. Aku berkata: 'Shalat, wahai Rasulullah.' Beliau bersabda: 'Shalat ada di depanmu.'

117.iwh_en: It was narrated that Abu Said al-Khudri said: 'Gold for gold, weight for weight.' He then met Ibn Abbas and said to him: 'What you say — did you find it in the Book of Allah or hear it from the Messenger of Allah ß?' Ibn Abbas said: 'I did not find it in the Book of Allah, but Usama ibn Zayd told me that the Messenger of Allah ß said: "Riba is only in credit transactions."'
117.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Abu Sa'id al-Khudri berkata: 'Emas dengan emas, timbangan demi timbangan.' Ia kemudian bertemu Ibn Abbas dan berkata kepadanya: 'Apa yang kamu katakan — apakah kamu menemukannya dalam Kitab Allah atau mendengarnya dari Rasulullah ß?' Ibn Abbas berkata: 'Aku tidak menemukannya dalam Kitab Allah, tetapi Usamah ibn Zayd memberitahuku bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Riba hanya ada dalam transaksi kredit."'

118.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ß say: "This (plague) is a punishment or torment that Allah sent upon those before you. It remains in some lands. When you hear it is somewhere, do not go to it; but if it breaks out in a land while you are there, do not flee from it."'
118.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Ini (wabah) adalah azab atau siksaan yang Allah kirimkan kepada orang-orang sebelum kalian. Ia masih ada di beberapa wilayah. Ketika kalian mendengar bahwa ia ada di suatu tempat, janganlah pergi ke sana; tetapi jika ia mewabah di suatu wilayah sementara kalian berada di sana, janganlah melarikan diri darinya."'

119.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'O Messenger of Allah, where will you stay tomorrow?' — during his Hajj. He said: 'Has Aqil left us any house?' Then he said: 'We will stay tomorrow, if Allah wills, at Khaif Bani Kinanah, where the Quraysh pledged unbelief.'
119.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, di mana kamu akan menginap besok?' — selama haji beliau. Beliau bersabda: 'Apakah Aqil meninggalkan untuk kami sebuah rumah?' Kemudian beliau berkata: 'Kami akan menginap besok, jika Allah menghendaki, di Khaif Bani Kinanah, di mana kaum Quraisy bersumpah atas kekufuran.'

120.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß would fast consecutive days until it was said that he would not break his fast, and he would not fast until it was said he would not fast. I never saw him fasting any month as completely as he fasted Sha'ban.'
120.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß berpuasa berhari-hari berturut-turut hingga dikatakan bahwa beliau tidak akan berbuka, dan beliau tidak berpuasa hingga dikatakan beliau tidak akan berpuasa. Aku tidak pernah melihat beliau berpuasa sebulan penuh seperti puasanya di bulan Sya'ban.'

121.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß when he entered the Ka'ba, prayed in all its corners and did not pray in it facing the door until he came out.
121.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß ketika memasuki Ka'bah, berdoa di semua sudutnya dan tidak shalat di dalamnya menghadap pintu sampai beliau keluar.

122.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ß became gravely ill, I came down from al-Awali and the people came down with me to Madinah. The Messenger of Allah ß would speak to them and lift a cloth from his face, and his words were: "May Allah curse those who take the graves of their prophets as places of worship."'
122.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ß sangat sakit, aku turun dari al-'Awali dan orang-orang turun bersamaku ke Madinah. Rasulullah ß berbicara kepada mereka dan mengangkat kain dari wajahnya, dan kata-kata beliau adalah: "Semoga Allah melaknat orang-orang yang menjadikan kuburan para nabi mereka sebagai tempat ibadah."'

123.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß departed from Arafat while Usama was his pillion rider. He was restraining his mount so much that its neck almost touched its chest, while saying: 'O people, be calm! Piety (birr) is not achieved by rushing.'
123.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß berangkat dari Arafat sementara Usamah adalah pemboncengnya. Beliau menahan tunggangannya sedemikian rupa sehingga lehernya hampir menyentuh dadanya, sambil bersabda: 'Wahai manusia, tenanglah! Kebaktian (birr) tidak diraih dengan tergesa-gesa.'

124.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß said: 'There is no riba in hand-to-hand transactions.'
124.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Tidak ada riba dalam transaksi tunai.'

125.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I entered upon Abd Allah ibn Ubayy during his illness with the Messenger of Allah ß, and the Messenger of Allah ß visited him and spoke to him. Abd Allah ibn Ubayy said: "You have preached enough. Leave us alone." The Prophet ß left. Then Abd Allah said to his son: "Go and ask him to seek forgiveness for me." So he went to the Messenger of Allah ß who then prayed for him and asked for forgiveness for him.'
125.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku masuk mengunjungi Abdullah ibn Ubay saat sakit bersama Rasulullah ß, dan Rasulullah ß mengunjunginya dan berbicara kepadanya. Abdullah ibn Ubay berkata: "Sudah cukup dakwahmu. Tinggalkan kami." Nabi ß pun pergi. Kemudian Abdullah berkata kepada putranya: "Pergilah dan minta beliau untuk memohonkan ampunan untukku." Maka ia pergi ke Rasulullah ß yang kemudian berdoa untuknya dan memohonkan ampunan untuknya.'

126.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß prayed inside the Ka'ba.'
126.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß shalat di dalam Ka'bah.'

127.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was the pillion rider of the Messenger of Allah ß on the evening of Arafah. When the sun set, the Messenger of Allah ß departed and put Bilal ahead of him. He walked at a measured pace, and when he found open space he would go faster. He continued like this until he reached Muzdalifa.'
127.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku adalah pembonceng Rasulullah ß pada sore hari Arafah. Ketika matahari terbenam, Rasulullah ß berangkat dan menaruh Bilal di depannya. Beliau berjalan dengan langkah yang teratur, dan ketika menemukan ruang terbuka beliau akan berjalan lebih cepat. Beliau terus seperti ini hingga tiba di Muzdalifah.'

128.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was the pillion rider of the Messenger of Allah ß on the evening of Arafah. When the sun set, he departed in a measured pace. When he found open space he would increase his speed, until he came to the mountain pass where people urinate. He descended and urinated, then I poured water for him. He then performed a brief ablution. I said: "Prayer, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Prayer is ahead."'
128.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku adalah pembonceng Rasulullah ß pada sore hari Arafah. Ketika matahari terbenam, beliau berangkat dengan langkah yang teratur. Ketika menemukan ruang terbuka beliau akan mempercepat langkahnya, hingga sampai di celah gunung tempat orang-orang buang air kecil. Beliau turun dan buang air kecil, kemudian aku menuangkan air untuknya. Beliau kemudian berwudhu singkat. Aku berkata: "Shalat, wahai Rasulullah." Beliau bersabda: "Shalat ada di depan."'

129.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ß say: 'There is no riba except in credit transactions (nasi'a).'
129.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Tidak ada riba kecuali dalam transaksi kredit (nasi'ah).'

130.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas asked him: 'What did you hear from the Messenger of Allah ß about plague?' He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ß say: "If you hear that it is in a land, do not go to it; but if it strikes a land while you are there, do not flee from it."'
130.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas bertanya kepadanya: 'Apa yang kamu dengar dari Rasulullah ß tentang wabah?' Ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Jika kalian mendengar bahwa ia ada di suatu wilayah, janganlah pergi ke sana; tetapi jika ia menyerang suatu wilayah sementara kalian berada di sana, janganlah melarikan diri darinya."'

131.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that Marwan ibn al-Hakam passed by him while he was praying, and Marwan imitated him. Usama then said: 'O Marwan, I heard the Messenger of Allah ß say: "Whoever prays a prayer and does not complete its bowing and prostration — that prayer is void, like a man who does not eat any food after eating nothing else."'
131.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Marwan ibn al-Hakam melewatinya sementara ia sedang shalat, dan Marwan menirunya. Usamah kemudian berkata: 'Wahai Marwan, aku mendengar Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang shalat tetapi tidak menyempurnakan ruku' dan sujudnya — shalatnya itu batal, seperti seorang pria yang tidak makan makanan apa pun setelah tidak makan sama sekali."'

132.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß combined the Maghrib and Isha prayers at Muzdalifa.
132.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß menggabungkan shalat Maghrib dan Isya di Muzdalifah.

133.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I said: "O Messenger of Allah, where will we stay tomorrow?" — during his Hajj. He said: "Has Aqil left us any house?" Then he said: "We will stay tomorrow, if Allah wills, at Khaif Bani Kinanah — the place where the Quraysh made a pact over disbelief."'
133.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, di mana kami akan menginap besok?" — selama hajinya. Beliau bersabda: "Apakah Aqil meninggalkan untuk kami sebuah rumah?" Kemudian beliau berkata: "Kami akan menginap besok, jika Allah menghendaki, di Khaif Bani Kinanah — tempat di mana kaum Quraisy membuat perjanjian atas kekufuran."'

134.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß rode a donkey with a saddle under which was a Fadaki cloth, and he carried Usama ibn Zayd behind him while going to visit Sa'd ibn Ubada.
134.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß menunggangi seekor keledai yang bersadel dengan kain Fadaki di bawahnya, dan beliau membawa Usamah ibn Zayd berbonceng di belakangnya saat pergi mengunjungi Sa'd ibn Ubadah.

135.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he informed his father Sa'd ibn Malik that a man came to the Prophet ß and was praising him. The Prophet ß said: 'Woe to you! You have cut the neck of your companion.' The Prophet ß said: 'If any of you must praise his companion, let him say: "I think so-and-so is such and such — and Allah is his reckoner — and I do not praise anyone above Allah."'
135.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia memberitahukan kepada ayahnya Sa'd ibn Malik bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Nabi ß dan memujinya berlebihan. Nabi ß bersabda: 'Celakalah kamu! Kamu telah memotong leher sahabatmu.' Nabi ß bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian harus memuji sahabatnya, hendaklah ia berkata: "Aku berpendapat bahwa si fulan adalah begini dan begitu — dan Allah adalah penghitungnya — dan aku tidak memuji seorang pun di atas Allah."'

136.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ß said: 'Jibril (peace be upon him) came to me and said: "Enter Paradise, you and everyone who carries in his heart even an atom's weight of faith."'
136.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ß bersabda: 'Jibril (alayhis salam) datang kepadaku dan berkata: "Masukkan ke dalam Surga, engkau dan setiap orang yang membawa dalam hatinya meski seberat zarrah keimanan."'

137.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I went to the Messenger of Allah ß and found him in a state of sadness. I asked him what was wrong. He said: "Jibril has not come to me for three nights." Then a young man appeared, and the Messenger of Allah ß said: "He is Jibril." He then revealed: "By the morning brightness..."'
137.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku pergi menemui Rasulullah ß dan mendapatinya dalam keadaan sedih. Aku bertanya kepadanya apa yang terjadi. Beliau bersabda: "Jibril tidak datang kepadaku selama tiga malam." Kemudian seorang pemuda muncul, dan Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Itu Jibril." Kemudian diturunkan: "Demi waktu matahari sepenggalahan naik..."'

138.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß said to me: "Bring my companions in to see me." They came in, and the Messenger of Allah ß uncovered his face and said: "O Allah, curse those who take the graves of their prophets as places of worship" — saying it three times.'
138.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß berkata kepadaku: "Bawa masuk sahabat-sahabatku untuk mengunjungiku." Mereka masuk, dan Rasulullah ß menyingkap wajahnya dan bersabda: "Ya Allah, laknatlah orang-orang yang menjadikan kuburan para nabi mereka sebagai tempat ibadah" — mengucapkannya tiga kali.'

139.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'One of the daughters of the Messenger of Allah ß sent to him (a message) saying that a child of hers — a boy or girl — was at the point of death. The Messenger of Allah ß sent back saying: "To Allah belongs what He takes and what He gives, and everything before Him has a fixed term. So be patient and seek reward."'
139.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Salah seorang putri Rasulullah ß mengutus seseorang kepadanya (dengan pesan) bahwa anaknya — seorang anak laki-laki atau perempuan — sedang sekarat. Rasulullah ß mengirim balasan: "Milik Allah apa yang Dia ambil dan apa yang Dia berikan, dan segalanya di sisi-Nya memiliki ajal yang tetap. Maka bersabarlah dan carilah pahala."'

140.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Ja'far, Ali, and Zayd ibn Haritha gathered together. Ja'far said: "I am the most beloved of you to the Messenger of Allah ß." Ali said the same, and Zayd said the same. They then went to the Prophet ß. Ja'far asked: "O Messenger of Allah, which of us is most beloved to you?" He said: "Fatimah." They said: "We are asking about the men." He said: "O Ja'far, you resemble me in character and in appearance; and Zayd, you are our brother and our client."'
140.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ja'far, Ali, dan Zayd ibn Haritsah berkumpul bersama. Ja'far berkata: "Aku adalah yang paling dicintai oleh Rasulullah ß di antara kalian." Ali berkata hal yang sama, dan Zayd berkata hal yang sama. Mereka kemudian pergi menemui Nabi ß. Ja'far bertanya: "Wahai Rasulullah, siapa di antara kami yang paling kamu cintai?" Beliau bersabda: "Fatimah." Mereka berkata: "Kami bertanya tentang kaum pria." Beliau bersabda: "Wahai Ja'far, kamu menyerupai aku dalam karakter dan penampilan; dan Zayd, kamu adalah saudara dan klienku."'

141.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ß said: 'Riba is only in credit transactions (nasi'a).'
141.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ß bersabda: 'Riba hanya ada dalam transaksi kredit (nasi'ah).'

142.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß was brought Umayma, the daughter of Zaynab, while her soul was rattling in her chest like water in an old leather container. The eyes of the Messenger of Allah ß overflowed with tears. Sa'd said: "What is this, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "This is mercy that Allah has placed in the hearts of His servants."'
142.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Dibawa kepada Rasulullah ß Umaimah, putri Zainab, sementara ruhnya bergejolak di dadanya seperti air dalam wadah kulit tua. Mata Rasulullah ß mengalirkan air mata. Sa'd berkata: "Apa ini, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau bersabda: "Ini adalah kasih sayang yang Allah tempatkan dalam hati hamba-hamba-Nya."'

143.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abu al-Sha'tha' said: 'I went on Hajj and entered the Ka'ba. When I was at the two pillars, I proceeded until I pressed against the wall. Then Ibn Umar came and stood beside me and prayed four rak'ahs.' He then asked him about that and mentioned that the Messenger of Allah ß had prayed inside the Ka'ba.
143.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abu al-Sya'tsa' berkata: 'Aku pergi haji dan masuk ke Ka'bah. Ketika berada di antara dua tiang, aku berjalan hingga menempel pada dinding. Kemudian Ibn Umar datang dan berdiri di sampingku dan shalat empat raka'at.' Ia kemudian menanyakannya tentang hal itu dan menyebutkan bahwa Rasulullah ß telah shalat di dalam Ka'bah.

144.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he used to travel to his property on Mondays and Thursdays. He was asked about that and he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß used to fast Mondays and Thursdays. I asked him about that and he said: "Deeds are presented to Allah on Mondays and Thursdays, and I love that my deeds be presented while I am fasting."'
144.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia biasa bepergian ke propertinya pada hari Senin dan Kamis. Ia ditanya tentang hal itu dan berkata: 'Rasulullah ß biasa berpuasa pada hari Senin dan Kamis. Aku bertanya kepadanya tentang hal itu dan beliau bersabda: "Amalan-amalan dipersembahkan kepada Allah pada hari Senin dan Kamis, dan aku suka amalan-amalanku dipersembahkan saat aku berpuasa."'

145.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß said: "I stood at the gate of Paradise and saw that most of those who entered it were the poor, while the wealthy were being detained. Those destined for the Fire had been ordered into it. I stood at the gate of Hell and saw that most of those who entered it were women."'
145.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Aku berdiri di pintu Surga dan melihat bahwa kebanyakan orang yang memasukinya adalah orang-orang miskin, sementara orang-orang kaya sedang ditahan. Orang-orang yang ditakdirkan untuk Neraka telah diperintahkan untuk memasukinya. Aku berdiri di pintu Neraka dan melihat bahwa kebanyakan orang yang memasukinya adalah wanita."'

146.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about the pace of the Messenger of Allah ß during the Farewell Hajj while he was present. He said: 'His pace was a brisk walk (anaq), and when he found open space he would accelerate to a nass — and the nass is faster than the anaq — while I was his pillion rider.'
146.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya tentang kecepatan perjalanan Rasulullah ß selama Haji Wada' sementara ia hadir. Ia berkata: 'Kecepatan beliau adalah langkah cepat (anaq), dan ketika menemukan ruang terbuka beliau akan mempercepat menjadi nass — dan nass lebih cepat dari anaq — sementara aku adalah pemboncengnya.'

147.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that it was said to him: 'Why don't you speak to Uthman?' He said: 'You think I am not speaking to him except so you can hear. I do speak to him privately, without opening a matter that I would not want to be the first one to open.'
147.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dikatakan kepadanya: 'Mengapa kamu tidak berbicara kepada Utsman?' Ia berkata: 'Kalian mengira aku tidak berbicara kepadanya kecuali agar kalian mendengar. Aku memang berbicara kepadanya secara pribadi, tanpa membuka suatu perkara yang tidak ingin aku jadikan orang pertama yang membukanya.'

148.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß sent me to a village called Ubna and said: "Come upon them in the morning and then burn it."'
148.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß mengutus aku ke sebuah desa yang disebut Ubna dan bersabda: "Datangilah mereka di pagi hari kemudian bakarlah."'

149.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ß clothed me in a thick Qubti garment that was part of what Dihya al-Kalbi had gifted him. I wore it, and the Messenger of Allah ß said: "What is the matter with you that you are not wearing the Qubti?" I said: "O Messenger of Allah, I gave it to my wife." He said: "Tell her to wear a lining beneath it, for I fear that it may describe the size of her bones."'
149.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ß memberi pakaian kepadaku berupa kain Qubti tebal yang merupakan bagian dari apa yang Dihyah al-Kalbi hadiahkan kepadanya. Aku memakainya, dan Rasulullah ß bersabda: "Ada apa denganmu sehingga kamu tidak memakai kain Qubti?" Aku berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, aku memberikannya kepada istriku." Beliau bersabda: "Katakan kepadanya agar memakai lapisan di bawahnya, karena aku khawatir kain itu menggambarkan ukuran tulang-tulangnya."'

150.iwh_en: It was narrated from Usama ibn Zayd (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Prophet of Allah ß used to take me and seat me on his thigh, and seat al-Hasan ibn Ali on his other thigh. Then he would embrace us and say: "O Allah, have mercy on them, for I show mercy to them."'
150.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Usamah ibn Zayd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Nabi Allah ß biasa mengambilku dan mendudukkan aku di pahanya, dan mendudukkan al-Hasan ibn Ali di pahanya yang lain. Kemudian beliau memeluk kami dan bersabda: "Ya Allah, kasihanilah mereka, karena aku mengasihi mereka."'
